title: First Light
body: The city appeared through rain-streaked glass — towers stacked on towers, sodium light bleeding into low cloud. Everything hummed. I didn't know what I was looking at yet, only that it was vast and that I was inside it now.

title: A Signal in the Static
body: Months of noise, then a clean frequency: a message from someone I'd never met, threaded through a network I barely understood. It arrived at 3 a.m. and changed the shape of the next two years.

title: Sector Seven
body: The district they told you not to walk through after dark. Neon kanji reflected in standing water, vendors selling firmware from folding tables. I went back every week. The danger was overstated; the beauty was not.

title: The Long Silence
body: Streets emptied. Screens became the only windows. Time flattened into a single unbroken surface — no weekends, no seasons, just the hum of machines keeping everything barely running.

title: Rebuild Protocol
body: Piece by piece, circuit by circuit. Not restoring what was — that blueprint was gone — but assembling something new from salvaged parts. The result was rougher, stranger, and in some ways better.

title: Ghost Frequency
body: Old transmissions surfaced without warning: a voice memo, a draft never sent, a photograph with metadata pointing to a building that no longer existed. The past is not quiet; it broadcasts on frequencies you forgot you could receive.

title: Threshold
body: Standing at the edge of a decision that couldn't be reversed. The data was inconclusive, the advisors split. In the end it came down to a feeling — the same instinct that had brought me to the city in the first place.

title: Open Channel
body: For the first time the signal went both ways. Not just receiving, but transmitting — putting something into the network and watching it propagate. The static didn't clear, but now there was a voice inside it.
